{{Hatnote|Not to be confused with [[Internet memes]]}}


Memes are pieces of transmissible [[phenotypic]] information.

While theoretically by that definition breast milk antibodies qualify, the term is in practice limited to rapidly transmissible information, cultural or otherwise. 

Memes are a critical component of the [[MGS model]] for [[Sapients|Sapient]] psychology. 

Memes are organized into [[memeplexes]] which are commonly transmitted together, and the sum of all memes in an organism is the [[memome]]. The study of memes is called [[memetics]], and is not to be confused with [[memeology]] which is the study of [[internet memes]].

[[memetic viruses]] are memes which spread at the expense of the [[biological fitness]] of the host. The definition is contentious as [[biological fitness]] is often ill defined in [[Sapients]].

[[Hydroplane|Hydroplanes]] are memes which keep people trapped in the [[Seven Plagues]]. They are sometimes characterized as [[memetic viruses]] and sometimes as 

[[Population memetics]] is an emerging discipline which will likely be critical in running the [[Church of Evolution]] and preventing the spread of [[Hydroplane|hydroplanes]].

== Can we create memes ==
It is known that humans can create [[genes]] and [[scenes]], but the idea of creation of memes, vs memes merely recombining in our minds is questionable. 

It is known that people are capable of directing memes to various targets with their own volition though

== Antihumanism of meme theory ==
While not readily apparent, meme theory puts into question basic ideas about human agency. Whether our thoughts are our own is put into question. 

== References ==
[[Category:Sapient Psychology]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Memes]][[sn:Memes]][[Category:MGS model]]
[[sn:Memes]][[Category:MGS roots]]
[[sn:Memes]]
[[Category:Pages]]
